pages.name: Home
pages.description: Marketing homepage introducing the AI chatbot SaaS and directing users to pricing and the app.
primary_functions: Explain the core value proposition of the AI chatbot product
Highlight key features such as multi-bot support, templates, analytics, and routing
Direct visitors to view pricing plans and choose a subscription
Provide a primary call-to-action to start using the product (leading to plan signup or dashboard)

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click primary 'View Pricing' or similar call-to-action
Click 'Go to Dashboard' for existing users
Click 'Learn more' about product capabilities

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Home' link in the main header navigation
Directly accessible as the root marketing URL

pages.name: Product Features
pages.description: Detailed overview of product capabilities across chatbots, templates, analytics, routing, and customization.
primary_functions: Explain major product modules: chatbots, templates, knowledge base, widget customization, routing, analytics, and lead capture
Show example use cases like e-commerce support and lead generation
Link users to pricing and plan signup once they understand capabilities
Support evaluation by outlining limitations and plan-based feature differences at a high level

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'See plans & pricing' after reviewing features
Click 'Read documentation' from feature deep-dive sections

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Product' link in the main header navigation
Linked from Home and other marketing pages

pages.name: Pricing
pages.description: Plan comparison page with monthly pricing cards and feature lists for each subscription tier.
primary_functions: Display all subscription plans with clear monthly prices and key limits (e.g., number of chatbots, analytics availability)
Allow users to compare plans and identify those under a specific budget (e.g., under $40/month)
Highlight which plans support at least a given number of chatbots and include conversation analytics
Provide primary action buttons on each plan card to continue to the plan signup form with the selected plan pre-chosen

incoming_params.description: Optional billing period preset for pricing toggle, e.g., 'monthly' or 'yearly'
Optional plan ID to visually highlight a recommended plan when arriving on the page

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the primary action button (e.g., 'Get Started' or 'Choose Plan') on a plan card
Click 'Contact sales' or 'Enterprise' link for custom plans

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Pricing' link in the main header navigation
Accessible via the 'Pricing' link in the global footer
Billing period and highlighted plan can be preselected via query parameters

pages.name: Plan Signup
pages.description: Plan-specific signup form to capture basic account details and confirm the selected subscription.
primary_functions: Show a summary of the selected plan (name, price, key limits such as chatbots and analytics)
Collect required user details including company name, email address, and password in a single form
Confirm creation of the account on the chosen plan and finalize subscription in one step
Redirect the user into the dashboard experience after successful account creation

incoming_params.description: ID of the selected PricingPlan to sign up for; used to load plan summary and create subscription

outgoing_connections.trigger: Successful form submission and subscription creation when createSubscriptionAndAccount.success is true and redirect_to_dashboard is true
Click 'Back to pricing' link or breadcrumb

access_methods.description: Accessed with a required planId query parameter from the Pricing page
Linked from plan cards and marketing CTAs that preselect a plan
Submitting the signup form triggers account and subscription creation

pages.name: Dashboard Overview
pages.description: Single-user dashboard home with high-level stats and shortcuts into key management sections.
primary_functions: Provide an at-a-glance overview of recent chatbot activity and key metrics
Offer quick access tiles or sidebar navigation to Chatbots, Routing, Analytics, and Team sections
Guide new users to create their first chatbot if none exist yet
Act as the central landing page after signup for ongoing product use

incoming_params.description: Optional flag such as 'new' to show a first-time welcome or onboarding state after signup

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View all chatbots' or select Chatbots from the dashboard navigation tiles
Click 'Create your first chatbot' when no chatbots exist
Click 'Configure routing' tile or link
Click 'View detailed analytics' from the metrics section
Click 'Manage team' tile
Click an onboarding link like 'Learn how to use the dashboard'

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Dashboard' link in the main header navigation
Optional 'welcome' parameter can be used to toggle onboarding UI
Users are redirected here after successful plan signup

pages.name: Chatbots List
pages.description: Management page listing all existing chatbots and providing entry points to create or configure them.
primary_functions: List all chatbots with basic information such as name, status, and key usage stats
Provide a primary action (e.g., '+ New Bot') to create a new chatbot and open the chatbot creation flow
Allow users to click a chatbot name to open its detailed settings page
Serve as the central hub for navigating between multiple chatbots in a single-user account

incoming_params.description: Optional chatbot ID to highlight in the list, e.g., after creation

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the '+ New Bot' button when starting from a template
Choose 'Create from scratch' option if provided
Click on a chatbot name in the list

access_methods.description: Linked from Dashboard overview tiles and internal app navigation
Optional highlightChatbotId parameter is accepted to focus a specific bot

pages.name: New Chatbot / Templates
pages.description: Creation flow for a new chatbot, including template browsing and initial configuration.
primary_functions: Offer creation options such as starting from scratch or using a pre-built template
Provide a template gallery with filters for categories and tags like 'customer_support' and 'e_commerce'
Allow users to select a template that matches both customer support and e-commerce needs and apply it
Collect initial chatbot setup details such as chatbot name, primary language, and greeting message before saving
Create the new chatbot and route the user to the chatbots list or that chatbots settings after completion

incoming_params.description: Determines whether the flow starts in 'from_template' or 'from_scratch' mode
Optional initial category filter for templates, e.g., 'Customer Support'
Optional tag filter for templates, e.g., 'e_commerce' or 'online_store'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Successful template-based creation when createChatbotFromTemplate returns redirect_to_chatbot_settings = true
Successful scratch-based creation when createChatbotFromScratch returns redirect_to_chatbot_settings = true
Return to list after creating a chatbot if redirect_to_chatbot_settings is false

access_methods.description: Mode and template filter can be preselected using query parameters
Accessed via '+ New Bot' from Chatbots List or onboarding CTA from Dashboard

pages.name: Chatbot Settings
pages.description: Comprehensive settings page for a single chatbot, organized into tabs (General, Knowledge Base, Appearance, Lead Capture, Members).
primary_functions: General tab: Edit core properties of the chatbot such as name and primary language
Knowledge Base / FAQs tab: Add, edit, categorize, and save FAQ entries and articles for the chatbot
Appearance / Widget tab: Customize widget color theme, position (including bottom-right), header title text, and visibility options, then save changes
Lead Capture / Forms tab: Enable lead capture, add fields like Full Name, Email, and Company, set required vs optional status, reorder fields, and save the form
Members / Collaborators tab: View and manage which team members can access this chatbot, add specific users (e.g., Emma Editor) as collaborators, and save permissions

incoming_params.description: ID of the chatbot whose settings should be displayed and edited
Optional tab identifier such as 'general', 'knowledge_base', 'appearance', 'lead_capture', or 'members'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to chatbots' from the settings header

access_methods.description: Requires chatbotId parameter; optional tab parameter controls which settings section is active
Accessed via clicking a chatbot name from Chatbots List or after creating a chatbot

pages.name: Routing & Automation
pages.description: Configuration page for business rules that control how conversations are routed between bot and human teams.
primary_functions: List existing routing and automation rules with their status and brief descriptions
Provide an action to add a new rule, opening a form for rule name, conditions, and actions
Configure time-based conditions such as business hours for specific weekdays and time ranges
Set actions that route conversations to a selected team (e.g., Sales) when conditions are met
Define fallback behavior for conversations that do not meet conditions, such as leaving them handled by the bot only, and save or enable the rule

incoming_params.description: Optional routing rule ID; when present, loads rule into the editor for editing
Optional editor mode such as 'create' or 'edit' to control whether a blank or existing rule is loaded

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click '+ New Rule' to open an empty rule editor
Click 'Edit' on an existing routing rule in the list
Click breadcrumb link back to Dashboard

access_methods.description: Linked from Dashboard overview and internal app navigation
ruleId and mode parameters control which rule is loaded into the editor

pages.name: Analytics & Reports
pages.description: Analytics dashboard showing conversation metrics with drill-down capabilities by date.
primary_functions: Provide a date range picker to select periods like the entire previous calendar month and apply filters
Display daily metrics including average messages per conversation for each day within the selected range
Allow users to sort daily metrics by specific values (e.g., average messages per conversation) in descending order
Enable users to identify the top day by average engagement and click that days row or data point to view more detailed breakdown on the same page
Support exploration of trends and performance over time to inform chatbot optimizations

incoming_params.description: Start date for the analytics range in ISO format, e.g., '2024-01-01'
End date for the analytics range in ISO format, e.g., '2024-01-31'
Analytics view mode; for this flow typically 'daily'
Optional chatbot ID to filter analytics to a specific bot; omitted or 'all' means all chatbots
Sort order for daily summaries, e.g., 'avg_messages_desc' to sort by average messages per conversation descending
ID of the selected DailyAnalyticsSummary whose detailed breakdown is displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Select 'Previous month' preset and apply date range filter
Click on a specific day's row or chart point to open its detail panel
Click breadcrumb link back to Dashboard

access_methods.description: Linked from Dashboard overview metrics and internal app navigation
Date range, view, sorting, and selected daily summary are all controlled via query parameters

pages.name: Team & Roles
pages.description: User and role management page for inviting team members and assigning access levels.
primary_functions: List all current team members along with their names, email addresses, and roles
Provide an 'Invite member' or '+ Add User' action to open a form for adding new users by email and name
Allow selection of roles such as Admin and Editor when inviting new members
Enable editing or removal of existing members if needed
Serve as the source of users that can later be granted access to specific chatbots via the chatbot Members tab

incoming_params.description: Optional team member ID to highlight, e.g., after inviting a new member

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click link or button such as 'Assign members to chatbots' to continue configuring access

access_methods.description: Accessible from Dashboard via 'Manage team' or similar navigation
Optional highlightMemberId can be used to scroll to and emphasize a specific user

pages.name: Help Center
pages.description: Support and documentation hub for learning how to use the AI chatbot platform.
primary_functions: Provide searchable documentation and FAQs about key features like chatbots, templates, routing, analytics, and lead capture
Offer categorized articles to help users self-serve common setup and configuration questions
Link to contact options or support channels for issues that require assistance
Assist new users in understanding how to complete common workflows represented in the tasks

incoming_params.description: Optional Help Center category slug to filter articles, e.g., 'chatbots' or 'analytics'
Optional search query string used to search Help Center articles
Page number for paginated help article search results

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit Help Center search form or click a category filter
Click 'Contact support' when documentation does not resolve an issue

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Help' link in the main header navigation
Search and category filters are represented as query parameters

pages.name: About
pages.description: Company background and mission information page.
primary_functions: Explain the companys mission, values, and story behind the AI chatbot product
Introduce the team or founders behind the product
Build trust with prospective customers evaluating the SaaS solution
Link back to key conversion pages such as Pricing and Contact

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View pricing' after reading about the company
Click 'Talk to us' or similar CTA on the About page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' link in the global footer
Can be accessed directly via marketing links or external references

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact page offering ways to get in touch with the company.
primary_functions: Provide a contact form for sales or support inquiries
Display alternative contact information such as email addresses or office locations
Allow prospective customers to request demos or ask pre-sales questions
Link to the Help Center for self-service resources

incoming_params.description: Optional topic preset for the contact form, e.g., 'sales', 'support', or 'billing'
Optional request type preset such as 'support' or 'demo'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Visit Help Center' for self-service before contacting support

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' link in the global footer
Topic and requestType can be preset via query parameters based on where the user came from
Submitting the contact form triggers submitContactRequest

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page describing how user and end-customer data is handled and protected.
primary_functions: Explain what data is collected from users and their customers
Describe how data is stored, processed, and shared
Outline user rights related to data privacy and compliance commitments
Support legal compliance and build trust for the SaaS product

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact us about privacy' link within the policy

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Privacy Policy' link in the global footer
Can be accessed directly from external links and compliance documents

pages.name: Terms of Service
pages.description: Legal terms governing use of the AI chatbot SaaS product.
primary_functions: Define the contractual terms between the service provider and the user
Explain acceptable use, limitations of liability, and termination conditions
Clarify payment, subscription, and renewal terms complementing the pricing page
Provide a reference for dispute resolution and governing law

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Contact us about these terms' for legal questions

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Terms of Service' link in the global footer
Can be accessed directly from external links and contracts

header_links.description: Homepage introducing the AI chatbot SaaS
Detailed overview of product features and capabilities
User dashboard for managing chatbots and settings

footer_links.description: Legal terms for using the service
